_patch_meta.description: VCB-11 VCSBFLAGS — 9 Session B observation flags raised during classification (DF-001, 003, 004, 005, 006, 007, 008, 009, 011). All concern borderline retentions or mediated inner-being engagements that Session B may wish to re-examine on closer interpretive review. No verse_context state changes; informational only.

record.description: G3392 (miaino) Joh 18:28: term names ceremonial-status defilement-avoidance ('they would not be defiled, but could eat the Passover'). Verse contains substantial inner-being content (irony / hypocrisy of ceremonial scrupulousness while plotting unjust execution), but that content is carried by narrative juxtaposition with v.30+, not by miaino itself which here names the ritual-status mechanism. Classifier retained relevance under section 3.3 borderline-retain. Session B may evaluate whether Joh 18:28 contributes substantively to the analytical profile of G3392 or is better treated as wrong_face on closer interpretive review.

operations.description: DF-001 — Session B flag for G3392 (label VCB11-SB-001)

record.description: G3878 (parakouo) Mar 5:36: lemma carries two senses — refuse to listen (Mat 18:17, classified relevant in 5111-001) and to overhear (Mar 5:36, set aside as wrong_face). The set-aside treats the overhearing-sense as carrying no inner-being content (sensory perception); verse's inner-being content is carried by phobeo (fear) and pisteuo (believe) in Jesus' response. Session B may wish to confirm that the homonym treatment is correct and whether the overhearing-sense warrants distinct registry treatment in any future programme work.

operations.description: DF-003 — Session B flag for G3878 (label VCB11-SB-002)

record.description: G3339 (metamorfoo) Mat 17:2 and Mar 9:2 (transfiguration narratives): group 1362-002 explicitly acknowledges the inner-being engagement as 'mediated' through the disciples' encounter — the term names Christ's outward transfiguration while the inner-being content (encounter with divine reality) sits in surrounding narrative (cloud, voice from heaven, fear, awe). The two-group structure of mti=1362 (1362-001 believer-transformation; 1362-002 Christ-transfiguration) makes the asymmetry visible. Session B may want to factor in that the registry's transformation profile draws on Christological events alongside believer-transformation verses.

operations.description: DF-004 — Session B flag for G3339 (label VCB11-SB-003)

record.description: H4906 (mas.kit) Eze 8:12: term = 'pictures' on the walls of private idolatrous chambers. Classifier read as the externalised form of inner imagination (each elder constructs his own mas.kit-chamber for private idolatry, externalising an inner conception). Distinguishing this case from Lev 26:1 / Num 33:52 (set aside as physical_only, public cult-stones) hangs on the personalised-vs-public dimension. Session B may wish to confirm that the personalised-imagination reading of Eze 8:12 is the right basis for the Session D synthesis on the imagination registry.

operations.description: DF-005 — Session B flag for H4906 (label VCB11-SB-004)

record.description: H2498 (cha.laph) Job 4:15: term names the spirit's motion past Eliphaz; the inner-being content (dread/awe) sits in the bodily reaction-noun ('hair of my flesh stood up') rather than in cha.laph itself. Group 1364-004 reads the term as 'engaging the person's inner capacity for perception or producing inner dread' — a mediated engagement. Session B may evaluate whether the term itself carries the dread or only marks the trigger-event.

operations.description: DF-006 — Session B flag for H2498 (label VCB11-SB-005)

record.description: H2498 (cha.laph) Job 11:10: newly classified in this session (no prior row); assigned to 1364-004 as related. cha.laph names God's passing-through in juridical-action mode; the inner-being engagement (creaturely-helplessness implied by 'who can turn him back?') is rhetorical / framing-mediated rather than directly term-borne. Fit with 1364-004 stretches the group's 'perception or dread' framing toward 'creaturely-helplessness' — a slightly different facet of the same characteristic. Session B may wish to assess whether the group description should be widened to embrace this verse explicitly, or whether the verse fits less well than its assignment suggests.

operations.description: DF-007 — Session B flag for H2498 (label VCB11-SB-006)

record.description: H2498 (cha.laph) Job 29:20: 'my glory fresh with me, and my bow ever new in my hand'. Assigned to group 1364-001 (inner renewal of strength via waiting/turning to God). Job 29:20 is Job's reminiscence of his former dignity and martial vigour — not a divinely-given inner renewal in the Isa 40:31 mode. Lexical sense (renewal) matches 1364-001 but the theological frame differs (self-referential vs God-given). Session B may evaluate whether Job 29:20 fits the group's characteristic-perspective description or represents a distinct facet (continuity of inner vitality vs God-given renewal).

operations.description: DF-008 — Session B flag for H2498 (label VCB11-SB-007)

record.description: G1761 (enthumesis) Acts 17:29: uses the term in the productive sense (the inner faculty by which a person devises wrong conceptions of God — idolatrous mental construction). Other three verses (Mat 9:4, Mat 12:25, Heb 4:12) use enthumesis in the receptive sense (inner content perceived/discerned by Christ / God's word). Classifier broadened the group description (917-001) to embrace both directions under one characteristic. Session B may wish to evaluate whether the productive-direction usage warrants its own analytical treatment, given its specific relevance to idolatry registries and to the broader question of how the inner imagination produces (rather than merely receives) content.

operations.description: DF-009 — Session B flag for G1761 (label VCB11-SB-008)

record.description: H2498 (cha.laph) 2Sa 12:20: 'Then David arose from the earth and washed and anointed himself and changed his clothes. And he went into the house of the Lord and worshipped.' Set aside as wrong_face — the verse is a powerful narrative pivot (David's transition from mourning to worship after the child's death). Inner-being content (David's theological reasoning, return to worship) is carried by the cluster of verbs (qum / rachats / suk / halaph / bo / shachah) and the surrounding narrative (vv.21-23 — David's articulation of the theological logic). Set-aside stands per second-look review. However, this is a candidate for Session B's wider analytical attention: the cluster of verbs all participate in the pivot, and Session B's analysis of registry 202 transformation may wish to position 2Sa 12:20 as a secondary witness to the term's narrative-pivot role even though it does not enter the registry's 202-transformation analytical pool.

operations.description: DF-011 — Session B flag for H2498 (label VCB11-SB-009)
